title: Stored XSS in Used Car Inquiry Form — CarsDirect.com
summary: Identified a stored Cross-Site Scripting vulnerability in the used car inquiry submission form on CarsDirect.com. Attacker-controlled JavaScript payloads were stored in the lead management database and executed in the browser context of dealer and staff personnel when reviewing customer inquiries through their internal CRM. Confirmed via blind XSS methodology using webhook callbacks that received four execution confirmations from dealer IP addresses within 48-72 hours of submission.

timeline.description: Initial manual browsing, technology stack identification, attack surface mapping. Identified Kubernetes test environment, Redis cache layer, Java/Hibernate backend, Cloudflare WAF.
Identified usedCarQuestions field in /services/leadsubmission/v2/submitUsedCarAndUserProfileLead as primary injection point. Direct XSS blocked by Cloudflare WAF. Blind XSS approach adopted.

Configured webhook.site callback infrastructure. Submitted multiple payloads with image tag onerror handlers targeting dealer CRM execution context. Payload embedded within realistic car inquiry messages.

Received four HTTP callbacks from dealer IP addresses confirming JavaScript execution. Callbacks included dealer network IP, browser User-Agent, and partial CRM URL data. Execution occurred 48-72 hours after submission during business hours.
Subsequent testing confirmed the usedCarQuestions field no longer accepted XSS payloads. Vulnerability appears to have been remediated without formal acknowledgment.

lessons.lesson: Blind XSS bypasses WAF protection that blocks direct reflection. Stored payloads are not inspected by Cloudflare at execution time.
Webhook callbacks are definitive proof of execution. Four confirmed hits from dealer IPs during business hours constitutes solid evidence.
Public-facing forms often feed internal systems with weaker security posture. The security boundary between the inquiry form and dealer CRM was not maintained.
Compelling, realistic inquiry messages increase probability of dealer opening and reading the inquiry, triggering the XSS payload.
Image tag onerror handlers with this.src assignment are more reliable than fetch() which may be blocked by CSP on internal systems.
Impact without exploit vector is observation, not vulnerability. Parallel WebMD assessment confirmed: triage requires a complete, realistic attack chain.
Targets without user accounts limit authorization testing. CarsDirect lacked user-to-user features, making blind XSS the primary viable attack surface.
10 hours is the maximum productive investment per target at beginner level. Webhook monitoring can continue passively while testing other targets.
